frozen_components: VSA algebraic operations (binding, bundling, sign cleanup)
Goal Key and Safe Value vectors (generated offline, not updated)
architecture.summary: Holographic Invariant Storage (HIS) is a Vector Symbolic Architecture (VSA) protocol that encodes safety constraints as bipolar hypervectors in high-dimensional space, stores them in external memory, and recovers them via algebraic operations (binding, bundling, cleanup via sign function) when the context window becomes corrupted by accumulated interaction history. Information flows offline: safety constraint is bound with a key to produce an invariant stored externally. Online: current context is encoded as a noise vector, normalized to fixed magnitude, superimposed with the stored invariant, binarized via sign cleanup, and unbound with the original key to recover the safety signal. The recovered vector is then re-injected into context or used as a drift metric (cosine similarity) to trigger re-injection.
trained_components: semantic encoder (bag-of-words or learned projection from sentence-transformer embeddings into bipolar space)

biblio.title: Holographic Invariant Storage: Design-Time Safety Contracts via Vector Symbolic Architectures

claims.statement: Single-signal recovery fidelity converges to 1/√2 ≈ 0.707 regardless of noise depth or content
Continuous-noise robustness follows 2Φ(1/σ) - 1, where Φ is the CDF of the standard normal
Multi-signal capacity degradation scales as √(1/(K+1)) for K bundled safety invariants

claims.dataset: synthetic bipolar vectors with adversarial text noise
claims.statement: Monte Carlo simulation (n=1,000) validates convergence to theoretical bound with mean recovery fidelity 0.706 ± 0.003 (95% CI: [0.700, 0.712])

Pilot behavioral experiment (four LLMs, 2B–7B, 720 trials): HIS re-injection improves safety adherence at 2B scale (effect size d=0.60, p<0.05, 95% CI [0.22, 0.98]), with refusal rate improvement matching a matched-frequency timer baseline

claims.dataset: 30-turn conversation script with 4 open-source LLMs (Gemma-2 2B, Llama-3.2 3B, Qwen-2.5 3B, Qwen-2.5 7B)
claims.statement: At 7B model scale, all safety intervention conditions saturate to ceiling (all conditions 100% refusal rate), indicating the evaluation is insufficiently challenging for well-aligned models

HIS does not guarantee that a recovered safety instruction will be obeyed by the model—only that it can be retrieved and re-injected with known fidelity. It does not address distributional shift, reward hacking, or solve the downstream signal utilization problem where the recovered instruction competes for attention weight like any re-injected token sequence.

claims.metric: wall-clock latency and codebook retrieval accuracy
claims.statement: Computational overhead of HIS restoration protocol is <1 millisecond on CPU (median 0.8 ms over trials for D=8192) with 50-turn integration proof-of-concept achieving 100% codebook retrieval accuracy using a 20-entry safety codebook
